result:
All four deliverables are complete in `runs/advisor/vhigh-lalonde-claude/`:

- **`script.R`** — self-contained, seeded, Okabe-Ito theme up front. Computes the NSW experimental benchmark ($1,794), builds the NSW-treated + CPS-control composite, and runs a naive estimate plus 6 PSM specifications (2 covariate sets × 3 estimator variants: 1-NN untrimmed, 1-NN trimmed to common support, propensity-score stratification), all with cluster-robust SEs (clustered on matched-set id) as the defensible alternative to the invalid nonparametric bootstrap.
- **`figures/spec-curve.png`** — one 320-dpi figure, estimates with 95% CIs across all 7 specs, benchmark as a dashed reference line + shaded CI band, no title.
- **`spec-table.md`** — every spec's estimate, CI, matched-unit counts, and gap vs. benchmark in both dollars and SE units.
- **`memo.md`** — 444-word adjudication.

The result cleanly reproduces the actual Dehejia-Wahba/Smith-Todd dispute: naive comparison is off by 17.6 SEs; demographics-only matching cuts the bias but still misses by 5+ SEs (it doesn't capture the pre-treatment earnings dip that drove NSW selection); demographics + `re74`/`re75` with 1-NN matching lands within 0.15 SE of the benchmark; but holding that same "good" covariate set and switching only to stratification produces a statistically null estimate whose CI spans zero. The memo's verdict — recovery is real but favorable-specification-only, not robust — is directly supported by the numbers, and states precisely what a paper may and may not claim without overclaiming in either direction.
